channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Chinese man living illegally in California tried to send weapons, ammo to North Korea: DOJ
item.description: Shenghua Wen, 41, was charged with conspiracy to violate the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, the United States Department of Justice said in a news release.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, NewsNation

item.title: Trump and Republican senators plan agenda for first 30 days
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump dialed in to what was described as a “love fest” Tuesday with Senate Republicans as they begin laying the groundwork for control of government in the new year, and a potential first-30-days agenda.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US border arrests drop 17% in November, dousing predictions of post-election surge
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — U.S. authorities made about 46,700 arrests for illegally crossing the border from Mexico in November, down about 17% from October to a new low for Joe Biden's presidency, an official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trial begins in the killing of an Ole Miss student who was known in the local LGBTQ+ community
item.description: A University of Mississippi student who disappeared in 2022 was a happy person who spoke with his mother daily and called her every year to sing happy birthday, his mom testified Tuesday in the trial of the man charged with killing her son.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chad Chronister, Donald Trump’s pick to run the DEA, withdraws name from consideration
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Chad Chronister, Donald Trump's pick to run the Drug Enforcement Administration, said Tuesday he was withdrawing his name from consideration, becoming the second person selected by the president-elect to bow out quickly after being nominated for a position requiring Senate confirmati
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Artist Jasleen Kaur wins Turner Prize for work exploring her Scottish Sikh identity
item.description: LONDON (AP) — An artist whose work exploring her Scottish Sikh identity includes a vintage Ford car draped in a crocheted doily won the U.K.’s prestigious Turner Prize on Tuesday, during a ceremony picketed by pro-Palestinian demonstrators.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: AI weapons detection system used in Tennessee schools sued by FTC
item.description: The technology scans students and other visitors for weapons, using AI, without requiring them to remove items from their person, according to previous marketing.
item.dc:creator: Tori Gessner, NewsNation

item.title: Organizations buy warming bus to help unhoused during winter
item.description: Two organizations have teamed up and purchased a school bus as a warming center for those without a home.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Harris, NewsNation

item.title: Trump vows to block Japanese steelmaker from buying US Steel, pledges tax incentives and tariffs
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is underscoring his intention to block the purchase of U.S. Steel by Japanese steelmaker Nippon Steel Corp., and he's pledging to use tax incentives and tariffs to strengthen the iconic American steelmaker.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump’s DEA nominee withdraws from consideration just days after being picked
item.description: President-elect Trump’s choice to lead the Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) announced on Tuesday he is withdrawing from consideration just three days after Trump made his nomination known.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What happened to Hannah Kobayashi? Police say the Hawaii woman voluntarily disappeared
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Hannah Kobayashi, a 30-year-old woman from Hawaii who was reported missing in Los Angeles three weeks ago, walked freely into Mexico, police said. Authorities declared Kobayashi a “voluntary missing person,” meaning she left of her own free will.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Justice Department announces sweeping reforms to curb suicides in federal prisons and jails
item.description: The Justice Department is ramping up efforts to address a crisis of suicides and systemic failures in federal prisons and jails, announcing sweeping reforms aimed at overhauling how mental health care is provided behind bars.
item.dc:creator: Michael Balsamo And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US 'seriously concerned' by developments in South Korea
item.description: The United States was surprised by South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol’s brief declaration of emergency martial law.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Defense makes closing argument in murder trial of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: Defense attorneys for a tech consultant charged in the stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee are making final arguments to jurors.
item.dc:creator: Janie Har, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Former Abercrombie CEO's partner pleads not guilty to sex trafficking charges, is freed on bond
item.description: CENTRAL ISLIP, N.Y. (AP) — Matthew Smith, the partner of former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO Mike Jeffries, was released on $10 million bond Tuesday in their sex trafficking case with conditions including home detention and GPS monitoring.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sinkhole may have swallowed Pennsylvania grandmother looking for missing cat: police
item.description: Authorities fear a Pennsylvania grandmother searching for her missing cat may have been swallowed by a sinkhole.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, ABC 4

item.title: Trump, DeSantis join thousands to mourn 3 Florida deputies killed in roadside crash
item.description: Thousands of people attended a memorial on Tuesday in West Palm Beach for three deputies who were killed in a roadside crash nearly two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jon Stewart mocks Dems, Fox News on Hunter Biden pardon
item.description: “The Democrats made this case an example of why Americans should believe in our system," Comedian Jon Stewart said.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Lt. General Chris Donahue's promotion approved by Senate
item.description: Lt. General Chris Donahue will now be the commander of the U.S. Army for Europe-Africa after his elevation was initially held up by Sen. Mullin.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Suspected Tren de Aragua gang member in Texas charged with murder, kidnapping
item.description: A suspect Tren de Aragua gang member in Texas is now facing murder and kidnapping charges following a death in San Antonio in July.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 8-year-old cancer survivor pushed down at Sunday's Bills-49ers game
item.description: The fun night was cut short after the family said a drunk Bills fan pushed Mia down the stairs in unshoveled section 312.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Morello, Fox 5

item.title: California teacher dies after suspected bat bite in her classroom
item.description: A central California middle school teacher died after contracting rabies from a suspected bat bite, local health officials said.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he would block US Steel acquisition by Nippon Steel
item.description: The Japanese firm announced last year that it would acquire U.S. Steel Corp. in a $14.9 billion deal — a move that was sharply criticized by lawmakers on both sides of the aisle.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Taco Bell adding photo booth experience to drive-thrus nationwide for a limited time
item.description: Select Taco Bell drive-thrus nationwide will be transformed into a “one of a kind” photo booth experience to capture fans’ everyday “Live Mas” moments. Taco Bell rewards members in California, Ohio, Tennessee, Texas and Florida will be able to participate in the drive-thru photo booth experience.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Here's the top 25 list of most-viewed Wikipedia articles of 2024
item.description: We collectively spent an estimated 2.4 billion hours reading English-language articles on Wikipedia in 2024, and we now know which were the most popular.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Adams, WGN 9

item.title: Supreme Court doesn't seem convinced FDA was unfair in blocking flavored vapes as teen use increased
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A majority of Supreme Court justices didn't seem convinced Monday that federal regulators misled companies before refusing to allow them to sell sweet-flavored vaping products following a surge in teen e-cigarette use.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dangerous man in custody in California for murder escapes, manhunt underway
item.description: A dangerous criminal in California Department of Corrections and Rehabilitation’s (CDCR) custody for murder escaped while being transported to the courthouse on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Don't miss these holiday shipping deadlines
item.description: The peak holiday shopping – and shipping – season is underway. If you want to make sure your gifts make it under the tree in time, you'll need to plan ahead.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida man accused of breaking into home, stabbing woman while she was sleeping inside
item.description: A Florida man was taken into custody after he allegedly broke into a woman's home, stabbed her while she was sleeping and attempted to flee from deputies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hannah Kobayashi listed as 'voluntary missing person' after video shows her crossing into Mexico: police
item.description: The Los Angeles Police Department said Hannah Kobayashi was seen on video crossing into Mexico from the U.S. on foot and there is no evidence of foul play in her disappearance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: These are the worst cities for package theft in 2024
item.description: The holiday season, which has already seen a surge in online shopping, presents even more opportunities for package thieves.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KTLA 5

item.title: Florida woman who left boyfriend in zipped suitcase for hours sentenced in his death
item.description: She was “shocked” by the guilty verdict and thought maybe the jury would convict her of a lesser charge, her lawyer said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KRON4

item.title: Wave of Democrats slam Biden's pardon of his son Hunter
item.description: While some Democrats extended a measure of sympathy, President Biden has taken heat on several fronts.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WGN 9

item.title: Prosecution makes closing argument in murder trial over stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Closing arguments began on Monday in the trial of a tech consultant in the 2023 stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee in San Francisco, an event that shocked the broader tech community whose members mourned the loss of an entrepreneur they called charismatic and kind.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US Postal Service touts timely delivery of mail ballots despite concerns from election officials
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service said Monday nearly 100% of completed mail ballots were returned to election offices within a week for this year's presidential contest, despite hurricanes, some misdirected election mail and delivery concerns raised by state officials.
item.dc:creator: DAVID SHARP and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What is 'lake-effect snow'? Warm air from large bodies of water is the key ingredient
item.description: When towns along the Great Lakes get buried in drifts of blowing snow, like several have over the past few days, weather experts start talking about the “lake effect." Lake-effect snow often occurs in relatively narrow bands that dump copious amounts of snow.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US Navy destroys Houthi missiles and drones targeting American ships in Gulf of Aden
item.description: U.S. Navy destroyers shot down seven missiles and drones fired by Yemen’s Houthi rebels at the warships and three American merchant vessels they were escorting through the Gulf of Aden. No damage or injuries were reported.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5
